unit.detail: 14 plugin suites green: run 346, compliance 966, security 470, iterate 476, adopt 387, grade 385, test 143, build 84, project 59, plan 57, deploy 26, changelog 23, design 19, preview 10

e2e.detail: F0.5 CLI surface: 38 tests via surface_verification.py --surface cli

degraded.detail: classify_complexity raised touches_auth from the DESCRIPTION prose (the word 'session'), not from the diff. No auth/RLS/middleware path is touched. Overridden; the diff-derived flag that actually binds is cross_component (hooks.json x8, **/hooks/*.py), whose integration coverage is delivered.

degraded.condition: pre-existing bug found by review, deliberately NOT fixed here
degraded.detail: trg-de6e736c: the 8 phase skills detect invocation mode from run_config.current_step, which the v2 lifecycle never advances (proven: after driving project+design through the real loop the frontier is 'plan' while current_step is still 'project'). NOT a regression from this iterate — the deleted phase_session_stop hook never wrote current_step either (verified against origin/main), so it was equally blind under multi_session. It is the long-standing v1-compat/v2 divergence and wants its own iterate across all 8 skills; bolting it onto a removal PR would be scope creep.

behaviors.behavior: The 6 engine modules (phase_session_start/_stop/user_prompt_validate, phase_context_blocks, hook_session, phase_event_emit) no longer exist
No live source imports a deleted module (a stale import is a hard load failure, not a type error)
No hooks.json registers a deleted hook (a stale registration makes Claude Code skip that plugin's WHOLE chain)
The 8 phase plugins no longer register the trio, and their UserPromptSubmit event is gone entirely (the validator was its only entry)
No live source or shipped skill emits a `claude --session-id` phase launch card
master_stop_check tells the user to re-invoke /shipwright-run instead of pasting a launch card
single_session is the sole mode; create_config always writes it and REJECTS the removed one
--mode no longer ADVERTISES multi_session, but passing it still yields the migration message (not `invalid choice`)
A non-drivable config (stale multi_session OR mode-less) is refused at the loop entry points (next/resume/gate/recover) with NO claim, NO mutation, NO event, NO loop-state
single-session-APPLY is refused too — it completes a task, plans a successor and appends a durable phase_completed, so guarding only `next` was security theatre
behaviors.evidence: test_non_drivable_config_guard.py + integration test_single_session_sole_mode.py::test_stale_multi_session_config_is_refused_with_no_side_effects (found by the internal code-review cascade)

behaviors.behavior: The ADVANCING generic lifecycle CLI commands (claim/complete/mark-failed/freeze-splits/plan-next-phase) are refused on a non-drivable config
behaviors.evidence: test_non_drivable_config_guard.py::test_advancing_lifecycle_command_refused_on_non_drivable_config (5 params) (found by the external GPT review)

behaviors.behavior: ...while the READ-ONLY lifecycle commands and recover-phase-task still work on a stale config (the migration itself calls recover)
A stale config still LOADS without raising (the guard is on the execution path, never the read path), so historical runs stay inspectable

INTEGRATION: a full 7-phase pipeline (plan/build fanning out over 2 splits) runs to status:complete over the real orchestrator CLI with the engine deleted — exercising claim, freeze_splits, complete and plan_next_phase without the Stop hook that used to call them
sessionUuid survives as the CAS claim token (not a Claude session id) — the task is claimed by its own sessionUuid
The crashed-phase escape hatch still composes: recover-phase-task -> re-claim on the BUMPED version -> complete -> pipeline advances, with the stale attempt CAS-fenced out
generate_handoff_on_stop still writes runtime/session_handoff.md after losing its phase-namespaced branch
...and its phase-completion fallback still fires (asserting only the handoff file would let a dropped fallback pass — the hook still exits 0 and still writes)

THE TRAP: phase gates stay INERT (interactive, should_stop) for a standalone / adopted / v1 / mode-less / corrupt / stale-mode config — removing the multi_session literal must not auto-arm the gate mechanism everywhere
...and gates still ACTIVATE for an explicit single_session run, so the guard is not over-broad
gate_policy.INERT_MODE is a sentinel, not a mode: it collides with neither single_session nor the removed literal

phase_started/phase_completed still land in the TRACKED events log, exactly one pair per (phase, splitId), driven through the REAL loop on a pipeline that actually fans out — the loop is now the SOLE producer
behaviors.evidence: test_single_session_sole_mode.py::test_loop_emits_one_tracked_start_end_pair_per_split (strengthened after the GPT review flagged the split-less version as vacuous)

behaviors.behavior: The per-split phase_completed dedup rule (iterate-2026-07-11) survives at its OWNER, record_event.py, driven as a real subprocess
behaviors.evidence: shared/tests/test_phase_completed_per_split_integration.py (re-homed: both emitters it used were deleted)

behaviors.behavior: The run SKILL documents a DRIVEN pipeline: master drives, phase-runner subagent, every surface, no mode question, no launch card, stale-config migration path
The v2 schema declares NO default for `mode` (absence is meaningful; a default would let an ajv-useDefaults consumer reinterpret a legacy config as drivable)
docs/hooks-and-pipeline.md, docs/guide.md, docs/gate-catalog.md and the migration note read correctly to a human
behaviors.evidence: Prose accuracy is judged, not asserted. The machine-checkable half IS pinned: no live source references a deleted module or renders a launch card (residue guard), and the run SKILL's contract is pinned by test_run_skill_drives_pipeline.py.

behaviors.behavior: The 11 Step-0 blocks across the 8 phase skills key on 'the orchestrator handed you a phaseTaskId', not the deleted SHIPWRIGHT-PIPELINE-CONTEXT block (whose only producer was phase_session_start) — otherwise a driven phase would silently take the standalone branch and contradict its own phase-runner brief
behaviors.evidence: integration-tests/test_multi_session_removal_residue.py::test_no_shipped_skill_gates_on_the_deleted_context_block (flagged independently by all three reviewers: internal, GPT-5.6, Codex)

behaviors.behavior: An EXPLICIT multi_session config is refused even without schemaVersion (a hand-edited config that lost it still meant a pipeline) — while a v1/standalone config, which also has no mode, is NOT told to set one
The lifecycle guard's rejection payload goes to STDOUT like every other lifecycle result, so a caller parsing stdout on a non-zero exit still finds it
The loop/apply module split is acyclic in both import orders and preserves behavior (apply_phase_result still reachable as single_session_loop.apply_phase_result)
behaviors.evidence: the whole run-plugin suite (346) imports both orders; test_single_session_loop.py + test_single_session_artifact_guard.py call loop.apply_phase_result unchanged

behaviors.behavior: The Step-0 retarget is guarded in BOTH directions: the dead SHIPWRIGHT-PIPELINE-CONTEXT trigger cannot return (negative), AND every one of the 8 phase skills still carries a phaseTaskId-keyed trigger + a get_phase_context.py call (positive) — a ban-only guard would still pass if someone deleted Step-0 outright, leaving the exact regression it exists to prevent
behaviors.evidence: integration-tests/test_multi_session_removal_residue.py::test_every_phase_skill_still_recovers_its_context_via_phase_task_id (8 params; FALSIFIED: goes red when Step-0 is stripped from a skill) (external review, GPT, delta round)

behaviors.behavior: The Step-0 retarget fixed BOTH branches: the 'you are in a pipeline' one AND the 'otherwise standalone' one. The first cut fixed only the positive branch — 8 skills still said 'if NO PIPELINE-CONTEXT block is present, you are standalone', so a DRIVEN phase would still have fallen into standalone mode. The guard missed it because it matched the LONG token while the stale text used the short one.
behaviors.evidence: integration-tests/test_multi_session_removal_residue.py::test_no_shipped_skill_gates_on_the_deleted_context_block, now matching the SHORT token (FALSIFIED: reintroducing the exact bug turns it red) — found by Codex in the delta review, after two earlier review rounds and my own guard had both passed it

behaviors.behavior: The Step-0 residue guard matches the dead trigger as a CLASS (case-insensitive, 5 spellings), not one exact token — a guard that matches a narrower string than the bug certifies exactly what it cannot see, which is how it missed the negative-branch blocker in the first place
behaviors.evidence: integration-tests/test_multi_session_removal_residue.py::test_no_shipped_skill_gates_on_the_deleted_context_block (FALSIFIED against 3 distinct re-wordings of the stale branch — all caught) (Codex, delta round #2)
